---
title: "Phenode_missingness"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-25"
---
# ---- Daily obs-count summary + time-series plots (VWC1, VWC2, LUX) for the recent wireless sensor data download ----
```{r}
# Assumes your CSV has columns:
#   Sensor
#   Time  (ISO8601 with Z, e.g. 2025-01-01T00:10:00.000Z)
#   TEROS-12-Calibrated VWC 1 (%)
#   TEROS-12-Calibrated VWC 2 (%)
#   Illuminance (lux)

library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)

# 1) Load data
df <- readr::read_csv("~/Downloads/wireless-sensor-data (12).csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

# 2) Rename key fields for easier handling
dat <- df %>%
  transmute(
    sensor = .data$Sensor,
    # parse as UTC then convert to Australia/Sydney, then take day
    datetime_local = ymd_hms(.data$Time, tz = "UTC") %>% with_tz("Australia/Sydney"),
    date = as_date(datetime_local),
    vwc1 = .data$`TEROS-12-Calibrated VWC 1 (%)`,
    vwc2 = .data$`TEROS-12-Calibrated VWC 2 (%)`,
    lux  = .data$`Illuminance (lux)`
  )

# 3) Daily observation counts
# If you also want to treat VWC == 0 as missing, set vwc_zero_is_missing <- TRUE
vwc_zero_is_missing <- TRUE

daily_counts <- dat %>%
  mutate(
    vwc1_obs = if (vwc_zero_is_missing) !is.na(vwc1) & vwc1 != 0 else !is.na(vwc1),
    vwc2_obs = if (vwc_zero_is_missing) !is.na(vwc2) & vwc2 != 0 else !is.na(vwc2),
    lux_obs  = !is.na(lux)
  ) %>%
  group_by(sensor, date) %>%
  summarise(
    n_vwc1 = sum(vwc1_obs),
    n_vwc2 = sum(vwc2_obs),
    n_lux  = sum(lux_obs),
    n_total_rows = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Optional: save summary
# readr::write_csv(daily_counts, "daily_observation_counts.csv")

# 4) Plot through time
# "all sensors and the three variables as separate lines":
# We'll reshape to long format so each sensor-variable combination is its own line.
plot_df <- daily_counts %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(n_vwc1, n_vwc2, n_lux),
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "n_obs"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    variable = recode(
      variable,
      n_vwc1 = "VWC 1 (Calibrated)",
      n_vwc2 = "VWC 2 (Calibrated)",
      n_lux  = "LUX"
    ),
    # Line grouping: each sensor is a distinct line within each variable panel
    sensor = as.factor(sensor)
  )

# Clear visualisation option A (recommended):
# Facet by variable -> each panel shows all sensors as separate lines.
ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = date, y = n_obs, group = sensor, colour = sensor)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.6, alpha = 0.9) +
  facet_wrap(~ variable, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Daily observation counts by sensor",
    x = "Date (Australia/Sydney)",
    y = "Number of observations per day",
    colour = "Sensor"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

# Clear visualisation option B (alternate):
# One panel per sensor, with the three variables as separate lines.
# (This is often even clearer when you have many sensors.)
 ggplot(daily_counts %>%
          pivot_longer(c(n_vwc1, n_vwc2, n_lux), names_to="variable", values_to="n_obs") %>%
          mutate(variable = recode(variable, n_vwc1="VWC1", n_vwc2="VWC2", n_lux="LUX")),
        aes(x = date, y = n_obs, colour = variable)) +
   geom_line(linewidth = 0.6) +
   facet_wrap(~ sensor, scales = "free_y") +
   labs(title="Daily observation counts per sensor", x="Date (Australia/Sydney)", y="Obs/day", colour="Variable") +
   theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
   theme(legend.position="bottom", panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
```

# --- Combining previously downloaded soil moisture file with new file to fill in gap around early June late May ---
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)

# --- file paths ---
f12 <- "~/Downloads/wireless-sensor-data (12).csv"
f9  <- "~/Downloads/wireless-sensor-data (9).csv"

# --- exact columns (edit if your headers differ) ---
time_col   <- "Time"
sensor_col <- "Sensor"

vwc1_12 <- "TEROS-12-Calibrated VWC 1 (%)"
vwc2_12 <- "TEROS-12-Calibrated VWC 2 (%)"

vwc1_9  <- "TEROS-12-Calibrated VWC 1"
vwc2_9  <- "TEROS-12-Calibrated VWC 2"

tz_use <- "Australia/Sydney"

# Helper: daily mean treating 0 as missing
daily_vwc <- function(df, time_col, sensor_col, vwc1, vwc2, tz) {
  df %>%
    mutate(Date = as.Date(ymd_hms(.data[[time_col]], tz = tz))) %>%
    transmute(
      Sensor = .data[[sensor_col]],
      Date,
      VWC1 = na_if(as.numeric(.data[[vwc1]]), 0),
      VWC2 = na_if(as.numeric(.data[[vwc2]]), 0)
    ) %>%
    group_by(Sensor, Date) %>%
    summarise(
      VWC1 = if (all(is.na(VWC1))) NA_real_ else mean(VWC1, na.rm = TRUE),
      VWC2 = if (all(is.na(VWC2))) NA_real_ else mean(VWC2, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}

# --- read and summarise daily ---
d12_daily <- readr::read_csv(f12, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  daily_vwc(time_col, sensor_col, vwc1_12, vwc2_12, tz_use) %>%
  rename(VWC1_12 = VWC1, VWC2_12 = VWC2)

d9_daily <- readr::read_csv(f9, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  daily_vwc(time_col, sensor_col, vwc1_9, vwc2_9, tz_use) %>%
  rename(VWC1_9 = VWC1, VWC2_9 = VWC2)

# --- build a complete daily scaffold ONLY within the time window of (9), per sensor ---
scaffold_9_window <- d9_daily %>%
  group_by(Sensor) %>%
  summarise(
    start_date = min(Date, na.rm = TRUE),
    end_date   = max(Date, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Date = map2(start_date, end_date, ~ seq.Date(.x, .y, by = "day"))) %>%
  select(Sensor, Date) %>%
  unnest(Date)

# --- join and fill: (12) within window of (9), fill missing dates/values from (9) ---
daily_12_filled_within_9 <- scaffold_9_window %>%
  left_join(d12_daily, by = c("Sensor", "Date")) %>%
  left_join(d9_daily,  by = c("Sensor", "Date")) %>%
  mutate(
    VWC1 = coalesce(VWC1_12, VWC1_9),
    VWC2 = coalesce(VWC2_12, VWC2_9),
    Filled_day_from_9   = is.na(VWC1_12) & is.na(VWC2_12) & (!is.na(VWC1_9) | !is.na(VWC2_9)),
    Filled_VWC1_from_9  = is.na(VWC1_12) & !is.na(VWC1_9),
    Filled_VWC2_from_9  = is.na(VWC2_12) & !is.na(VWC2_9)
  ) %>%
  arrange(Sensor, Date) %>%
  select(
    Sensor, Date,
    VWC1, VWC2,
    VWC1_12, VWC2_12,
    VWC1_9,  VWC2_9,
    Filled_day_from_9, Filled_VWC1_from_9, Filled_VWC2_from_9
  )

 ggplot(daily_12_filled_within_9 %>%
          pivot_longer(c(n_vwc1, n_vwc2, n_lux), names_to="variable", values_to="n_obs") %>%
          mutate(variable = recode(variable, n_vwc1="VWC1", n_vwc2="VWC2", n_lux="LUX")),
        aes(x = date, y = n_obs, colour = variable)) +
   geom_line(linewidth = 0.6) +
   facet_wrap(~ sensor, scales = "free_y") +
   labs(title="Daily observation counts per sensor", x="Date (Australia/Sydney)", y="Obs/day", colour="Variable") +
   theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
   theme(legend.position="bottom", panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

# --- write output ---
readr::write_csv(daily_12_filled_within_9, "daily_vwc_12_filled_from_9_within_9_window.csv")
```

# ---- Fill gaps between two weather-station datasets (ANU 4 + ANU 3) ----
```{r}

# Goal: create a single "best-available" time series where missing values in ANU 4
# are filled from ANU when timestamps align (or after resampling to a common interval).
#
# Assumptions:
# - Both CSVs have a "Time" column in UTC ISO8601 with trailing "Z"
# - Variables largely match by name (some PhenoDE-only fields like Primary temp)
# - You want: air temp, pressure, rain, RH, VPD, windspeed
#
# Strategy:
# 1) Parse timestamps
# 2) Resample both to a common time grid (e.g. 10-min or hourly)
# 3) "Coalesce" (prefer PhenoDE, fill from ANU)
# 4) Recompute VPD on the combined series (recommended)

library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)

# ---- File paths ----
phenode_path <- "~/Downloads/ANU_4_All_Phenode_Data/phenode_sensor_data.csv"
anu_path     <- "~/Downloads/ANU_3_Environmental_Data 4.csv"

# ---- Choose common time step ----
# Use "10 mins" if your station logs frequently; "1 hour" is safer for joins.
time_step <- "24 hours"

# ---- Variables to use (edit if needed) ----
# Note: ANU file appears to have "Air Temperature Secondary (°C)" but not "Air Temperature Primary (°C)"
temp_col_anu      <- "Air Temperature Secondary (°C)"
temp_col_phenode  <- "Air Temperature Primary (°C)"  # fallback to secondary if primary missing
temp_col_phenode2 <- "Air Temperature Secondary (°C)"

press_col <- "Atmospheric Pressure (kPa)"
rain_col  <- "Calculated Fallen Rain (mm)"
rh_col    <- "Relative Humidity (%)"
vp_col    <- "Vapor Pressure (kPa)"
wind_col  <- "Wind Speed (m/s)"

# ---- Helper: aggregate to regular grid ----
# For most met vars: mean across the interval
# For rainfall: SUM across the interval (if it's incremental per record)
# If "Calculated Fallen Rain (mm)" is already an interval total, summing is appropriate;
# if it's cumulative, you'd need a different approach.
aggregate_to_grid <- function(df, source_name) {
  df %>%
    mutate(
      Time = ymd_hms(Time, tz = "UTC"),
      # Create time bins
      t_bin = floor_date(Time, unit = time_step)
    ) %>%
    group_by(t_bin) %>%
    summarise(
      source = source_name,
      # Temperature: prefer primary if present; else secondary; else NA
      temp_primary = if ("Air Temperature Primary (°C)" %in% names(df)) mean(`Air Temperature Primary (°C)`, na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_,
      temp_secondary = if ("Air Temperature Secondary (°C)" %in% names(df)) mean(`Air Temperature Secondary (°C)`, na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_,
      pressure = if (press_col %in% names(df)) mean(.data[[press_col]], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_,
      rh       = if (rh_col %in% names(df)) mean(.data[[rh_col]], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_,
      vp       = if (vp_col %in% names(df)) mean(.data[[vp_col]], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_,
      wind     = if (wind_col %in% names(df)) mean(.data[[wind_col]], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_,
      rain     = if (rain_col %in% names(df)) sum(.data[[rain_col]], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_,
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    # Fix na.rm = TRUE behaviour: mean/sum returns Inf/-Inf when all NA
    mutate(across(c(temp_primary, temp_secondary, pressure, rh, vp, wind, rain),
                  ~ ifelse(is.infinite(.x), NA_real_, .x)))
}

# ---- Load + aggregate both ----
phenode_raw <- readr::read_csv(phenode_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
anu_raw     <- readr::read_csv(anu_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

phenode_g <- aggregate_to_grid(phenode_raw, "phenode")
anu_g     <- aggregate_to_grid(anu_raw,     "anu")

# ---- Combine using coalesce (prefer PhenoDE, fill from ANU) ----
# Temperature: use PhenoDE primary if available, else PhenoDE secondary, else ANU secondary
combined <- full_join(
  phenode_g %>% select(-source),
  anu_g     %>% select(-source),
  by = "t_bin",
  suffix = c("_phenode", "_anu")
) %>%
  transmute(
    datetime_utc = t_bin,

    # Temp: prefer phenode primary -> phenode secondary -> anu secondary
    air_temp_C = coalesce(temp_primary_phenode, temp_secondary_phenode, temp_secondary_anu),

    atmospheric_pressure_kPa = coalesce(pressure_phenode, pressure_anu),
    relative_humidity_pct    = coalesce(rh_phenode, rh_anu),
    vapor_pressure_kPa       = coalesce(vp_phenode, vp_anu),
    wind_speed_ms            = coalesce(wind_phenode, wind_anu),

    # Rain: if phenode rain missing, use ANU. If BOTH have values and you believe they are independent
    # measures of the same thing, you should NOT add them (would double-count). Coalesce is safest.
    fallen_rain_mm           = coalesce(rain_phenode, rain_anu)
  ) %>%
  arrange(datetime_utc)

# ---- Recompute VPD on the combined series (recommended) ----
# VPD = es(T) - ea; if you have vapor_pressure_kPa as actual vapor pressure (ea), this works.
# Tetens saturation vapor pressure (kPa)
combined <- combined %>%
  mutate(
    es_kPa = 0.6108 * exp((17.27 * air_temp_C) / (air_temp_C + 237.3)),
    vpd_kPa = es_kPa - vapor_pressure_kPa
  )

# ---- OPTIONAL: If vapor pressure is missing but RH is present, estimate ea from RH ----
# ea = (RH/100) * es
combined <- combined %>%
  mutate(
    vapor_pressure_kPa = if_else(
      is.na(vapor_pressure_kPa) & !is.na(relative_humidity_pct) & !is.na(es_kPa),
      (relative_humidity_pct / 100) * es_kPa,
      vapor_pressure_kPa
    ),
    vpd_kPa = es_kPa - vapor_pressure_kPa
  )

# ---- Quick missingness check after filling ----
missing_summary <- combined %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    pct_missing_temp  = mean(is.na(air_temp_C)) * 100,
    pct_missing_press = mean(is.na(atmospheric_pressure_kPa)) * 100,
    pct_missing_rh    = mean(is.na(relative_humidity_pct)) * 100,
    pct_missing_vp    = mean(is.na(vapor_pressure_kPa)) * 100,
    pct_missing_vpd   = mean(is.na(vpd_kPa)) * 100,
    pct_missing_wind  = mean(is.na(wind_speed_ms)) * 100,
    pct_missing_rain  = mean(is.na(fallen_rain_mm)) * 100
  )

print(missing_summary)

# ---- Save combined dataset ----
readr::write_csv(combined, "weather_filled_combined.csv")


```
